pattern: Only the first half of a palindrome is free to choose, so sorting that half ascending and mirroring it yields the unique lexicographically smallest arrangement.
hints: A palindrome is fully determined by its left half plus an optional single middle character.
Each letter contributes count // 2 copies to the left half; a letter with an odd count must sit in the exact middle.
Sort the multiset of half-copies ascending to form the left half, place the odd letter (if any) in the centre, and append the reverse of the left half.

edge_inputs.description: single character is its own palindrome
odd length with a forced middle letter
largest letters must move to the outside-in order
many distinct letters with one odd

stress.description: random palindromes built from a random multiset so the smallest rearrangement is non-trivial
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import random
    half_len = max(1, size // 2)
    half = [chr(ord('a') + rng.randrange(6)) for _ in range(half_len)]
    mid = chr(ord('a') + rng.randrange(6)) if rng.random() < 0.5 else ''
    rng.shuffle(half)
    s = ''.join(half) + mid + ''.join(reversed(half))
    return [s]
stress.note: construction guarantees the input is a palindrome as required
